idea.title: Limelight
idea.hook: Move one spotlight across a plaza; whoever you light adopts a mood that spreads to their neighbors — orchestrate the whole crowd into joy before panic infects them.
idea.hook_ru: Веди один прожектор по площади; кого осветишь — у того настроение, и оно передаётся соседям. Зажги всю толпу радостью, пока её не охватила паника.
idea.prototype: Turn-based mood-contagion puzzle on a plaza grid of stylised crowd figures. You control ONE spotlight (tap a tile to move it; tap a person to move onto them AND light them = joy). Lighting a person spends one move and triggers a spread turn: every neutral person orthogonally adjacent to a joyful one becomes joyful, adjacent to a panicked one becomes panicked, and joy wins contested tiles. Empty plaza tiles block the spread. WIN a level when joyCount reaches the joy target (85% on big grids, 100% on tiny ones) before either lose trigger. LOSE when panicCount reaches 60% of the crowd, or moves run out / no neutral people remain with the goal unmet. 8 hand-tuned levels with rising grid size + more panic seeds; move-count star rating (3 stars = fewest moves). Mobile-portrait-first; every tile is a thumb-sized tap target.
idea.mutation_of: market-radar daily-mix market_anchor (cellular-automaton / infection-spread puzzle in the puzzle_sort_match family)
idea.why_novel: Most sort/match puzzles reward grouping like-with-like on a static board; Limelight makes the board ALIVE — two moods race outward as simultaneous contagions and the player's only lever is where to plant a single joy seed each turn. It is an infection/cellular-automaton puzzle (closer to a one-piece reversi-of-moods) wrapped in the readable, tactile puzzle_objects cover style the portals reward, which the sort/match cluster on Yandex never does.
idea.non_timer_pressure: moves budget AND panic-spread contagion (two non-timer axes, no countdown clock)
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when panicCount >= ceil(total * PANIC_LOSE_FRAC) (default 60% of the crowd in panic), OR when movesLeft <= 0 with joyCount < target, OR when no neutral people remain and the joy goal is unmet (fate sealed).

sources.name: market-radar opportunities 2026-05-26 (#3 Limelight, prototype_only, 73.7)
sources.note: SIGNAL pick. Scores: web 72.8, mobile transfer 83.5, CTR 52, execution 90.0, novelty 72.0. Verdict prototype_only — outside-in bet on a contagion-spread puzzle in the puzzle_sort_match family.

sources.name: Merge Dale
sources.note: CTR reference — puzzle_objects archetype, large tactile objects, clear color groups.

sources.name: Block Dropping Merge
sources.note: CTR reference — object scale + one obvious near-solved move.

sources.name: Vega Mix 2
sources.note: CTR reference — saturated tactile objects, high contrast.

synthesizer_brief: Daily MARKET SIGNAL slot, mechanic-led. Took market-radar opportunity #3 (Limelight, puzzle_sort_match family, puzzle_objects CTR archetype) and built an infection/cellular-automaton puzzle: one spotlight, a single joy seed per turn, two moods (joy + panic) racing across an orthogonal-neighbour spread on a plaza grid, with empty tiles as walls. Pressure is non-timer on two axes — a strict moves budget AND panic contagion that loses at 60% crowd panic. Portfolio data says players abandon puzzles that are 'unclear what to do', so level 1 is trivially solvable (3 figures in a row, light the centre = win in one move) to teach the spread rule by doing, with a live joyCount/target smile counter and a pulsing panic warning. 8 hand-tuned, beam-search-verified-solvable levels with rising grid + more (mostly gap-boxed) panic seeds, plus a fewest-moves star rating for depth. Cover borrows object scale / contrast / clear colour groups from the puzzle_objects refs (Merge Dale, Block Dropping Merge, Monster Match, Vega Mix 2): a bright spotlight beam turning a grid of grey panicked figures gold, one obvious near-solved cell left.

alternatives.title: Leak Run
alternatives.hook: You're the lone attacker sneaking past the towers — read their firing cones and step one tile at a time through the gaps to reach the throne.
alternatives.why_novel: Turn-based stealth-through-firing-cones on a grid; reads the defender as a pattern-puzzle rather than a twitch dodger. NOT built today.

alternatives.title: Reagent
alternatives.hook: Drop a strictly limited set of element emitters and let the reactions cascade to convert the board to a target element — pick the wrong reagent and you've wasted your last drop.
alternatives.why_novel: Resource-scarce chain-reaction board conversion; one wrong placement is fatal, so it's a planning puzzle not a clicker. NOT built today.

alternatives.title: Heave
alternatives.hook: Shoulder a boulder up a crumbling slope by timing rhythmic shoves against gravity, banking rest on ledges — slip once and it rolls back over you.
alternatives.why_novel: Rhythm-vs-gravity climbing with rest-banking on ledges; physical pressure (gravity) instead of a timer. NOT built today.

alternatives.title: Drop Zone
alternatives.hook: Plant a handful of charges and set their detonation ORDER so the tower folds and collapses neatly into the dumpster — not onto the neighbors.
alternatives.why_novel: Demolition-ordering puzzle: the sequence of detonations, not the placement alone, decides success. NOT built today.

alternatives.title: Sum Lane
alternatives.hook: Steer your number-blob down lanes of x, +, and - gates to land on the EXACT target — overshoot by one and the vault stays locked.
alternatives.why_novel: Arithmetic-gate runner where the WIN is an exact value, punishing overshoot — math precision as the pressure. NOT built today.
